data.title: ChatGPT Prompts for Event Planners
data.metaTitle: 15 ChatGPT Prompts for Event Planners (Marketing & Client) 2026
data.metaDescription: ChatGPT prompts for event planners and coordinators to write proposals, venue outreach, client emails, vendor briefs, and marketing content. Win more events.
data.keywords: chatgpt prompts for event planners, event planning marketing prompts ai, event coordinator email templates chatgpt, event business prompts
data.heading: 15 ChatGPT Prompts for Event Planners That Win More Events and Wow More Clients
data.intro: Event planning is relationship management at scale — vendors, venues, clients, and guests all need different communication. These prompts give you the templates for every scenario, from the first inquiry to the final thank-you.

prompts.title: Event proposal (client-facing)
prompts.prompt: Write an event proposal for [CLIENT NAME]'s [EVENT TYPE] with approximately [GUEST COUNT] guests on [DATE] at [VENUE/LOCATION]. Include: event concept overview, proposed timeline, key vendors and services included, investment breakdown (broad line items), why our planning firm is the right choice (3 differentiators), and CTA to schedule a planning call. Under 500 words. Professional, creative.
prompts.tip: Event proposals that paint a picture of the finished event (not just a service list) win more business. Sell the vision, not the logistics.

prompts.title: Venue inquiry email
prompts.prompt: Write an email to [VENUE NAME] inquiring about availability and pricing for a [EVENT TYPE] on [PREFERRED DATE(S)] for approximately [GUEST COUNT] guests. Include: event description (brief), setup requirements ([CEREMONY/DINNER/CORPORATE SETUP]), catering question (in-house or outside vendors allowed?), budget range (if comfortable sharing), and a request for a site visit. Under 200 words.
prompts.tip: Venue inquiry emails that include a budget range get faster, more relevant quotes — venues know whether to pitch their premium or standard package.

prompts.title: Vendor brief (photographer/catering/band)
prompts.prompt: Write a vendor brief for [VENDOR TYPE — photographer/catering/entertainment] for [EVENT NAME] on [DATE]. Include: event overview (type, location, guest count), specific requirements for this vendor (5-6 bullet points), timeline they need to work within, how they'll coordinate with me, and budget range. Professional, organized. Under 250 words.
prompts.tip: Detailed vendor briefs produce accurate quotes on the first try and prevent surprises on the day.

prompts.title: Client onboarding email (new booking)
prompts.prompt: Write an onboarding email to new client [NAME] who just booked [PLANNING PACKAGE] for their [EVENT TYPE] on [DATE]. Include: excitement and congratulations, what happens next (3 steps in the first 2 weeks), what they need to prepare or decide, and how to reach you. Under 200 words. Organized, warm, confidence-building.
prompts.tip: Clients who receive a clear onboarding plan make faster decisions and generate fewer anxious check-in calls.

prompts.title: Day-of timeline template (event run sheet)
prompts.prompt: Create a detailed day-of timeline/run sheet for a [EVENT TYPE] with [GUEST COUNT] guests at [VENUE TYPE]. Include time slots from [START TIME] to [END TIME] with: setup windows, vendor arrival times, key event milestones ([CEREMONY/DINNER/SPEECHES/DANCING etc.]), buffer times for transitions, and responsible party for each item. Editable format.
prompts.tip: Day-of timelines with 10-15 minute buffers between key transitions prevent the cascading delays that define stressful events.

prompts.title: Social media post (event highlight)
prompts.prompt: Write 3 Instagram caption variations for a highlight post from a recent [EVENT TYPE] at [VENUE/CITY]. Version 1: venue/atmosphere focused. Version 2: client story (with permission). Version 3: behind-the-scenes planning moment. 3-5 relevant hashtags each. Under 200 words per caption. Evocative and emotional — events are about feelings.
prompts.tip: Event highlight posts with an emotional angle ('this is why we do what we do') outperform vendor-tag posts in reach and saves.

prompts.title: Email to non-converting inquiry (follow-up)
prompts.prompt: Write a follow-up email to an event inquiry from [CLIENT NAME] who hasn't responded in [X] days. Re-express your enthusiasm for their event, ask if they have any questions about the proposal, and offer to adjust the scope or schedule a brief call. Not pushy. Under 100 words. One last genuine outreach.
prompts.tip: 50% of event bookings happen after 2+ follow-up touches. One follow-up email after silence is almost always worth sending.

prompts.title: Post-event wrap-up email (to client)
prompts.prompt: Write a post-event wrap-up email to client [NAME] after their [EVENT TYPE] on [DATE]. Include: genuine reflection on how the day went, what you're proud of about the event, any final administrative notes (vendor invoices, final photo/video timelines), and an invitation to leave a review or refer a friend. Under 200 words. Warm, closing a successful chapter.
prompts.tip: Post-event emails that reflect on a specific memorable moment feel personal and generate the most genuine Google/social reviews.

prompts.title: Referral request (to past client)
prompts.prompt: Write an email to past client [NAME] whose [EVENT TYPE] you planned in [YEAR]. Express continued appreciation for the experience, reference a specific detail you loved about their event, and let them know you'd welcome a referral if they know anyone planning a [WEDDING/CORPORATE EVENT/BIRTHDAY] in the next year. Under 120 words. Genuine, relationship-first.
prompts.tip: Event planning referrals from past clients convert at 60%+ — no other marketing comes close. Every past client relationship is worth maintaining.

prompts.title: Styled shoot invitation (to vendors)
prompts.prompt: Write an invitation to [VENDOR TYPE — photographer, florist, caterer] to participate in a styled shoot for [EVENT TYPE] planned by [YOUR COMPANY NAME] on [DATE] at [LOCATION]. Cover: the concept and aesthetic, what vendors will receive (images for portfolio, social features, credits), what they need to contribute, and logistics. Under 250 words. Collaborative, exciting.
prompts.tip: Styled shoots create portfolio content that attracts ideal clients — the ROI is measured in the quality of future inquiries, not cash.

prompts.title: Preferred vendor list pitch (to venue)
prompts.prompt: Write an email to [VENUE NAME]'s events coordinator requesting inclusion on their preferred vendor list as an event planner. Include: brief description of your services and planning style, 2-3 types of events you specialize in, how being on their preferred list benefits their clients, any events you've already hosted there (if applicable). Under 200 words. Professional, mutually beneficial framing.
prompts.tip: Venue preferred vendor relationships are the most sustainable lead source for event planners — invest in them like partnerships.

prompts.title: Wedding ceremony script (civil non-religious)
prompts.prompt: Write a simple, modern civil wedding ceremony script for [COUPLE NAMES] on [DATE]. Include: officiant welcome (1-2 minutes), brief acknowledgment of guests, declaration of intent (2 exchanges), vows (2 short, adaptable vow options), ring exchange, pronouncement, and first kiss/presentation. Tone: warm, slightly poetic, inclusive. Under 500 words total. Customizable brackets [LIKE THIS] for personal details.
prompts.tip: Having a ceremony script draft ready for couples who don't know where to start is an unexpected differentiator — most planners don't offer this.

prompts.title: Sponsorship package for industry event
prompts.prompt: Write a sponsorship package for [EVENT NAME], a [TYPE OF INDUSTRY/COMMUNITY EVENT] organized by [COMPANY/ORGANIZATION] on [DATE]. Include 3 tiers: Gold ($[X]), Silver ($[Y]), Bronze ($[Z]). For each tier: list of benefits (brand exposure, speaking time, attendee list access, etc.), and brief positioning statement. Add an 'a la carte' options section. Under 400 words.
prompts.tip: Event sponsorship packages with 3 tiers attract more total sponsorship dollars than single-tier offerings — give sponsors options to fit their budget.

prompts.title: Guest communication email (event day logistics)
prompts.prompt: Write a guest communication email for attendees of [EVENT NAME] on [DATE] at [VENUE]. Include: arrival time guidance, parking/transport instructions, dress code (if applicable), what to bring (ticket/registration confirmation), schedule highlights, and contact for day-of questions. Under 200 words. Clear, organized, reduces guest anxiety.
prompts.tip: Day-of logistics emails sent 48 hours before the event reduce 'where should I park?' questions by 70%.

prompts.title: Google Business / directory profile description
prompts.prompt: Write a business description for [EVENT PLANNING COMPANY] for Google Business, Yelp, or The Knot. Cover: types of events planned, service area, planning style/specialties, what clients say about working with you (one testimonial paraphrase), and how to inquire. Under 200 words. Keyword-rich: include '[EVENT TYPE] planner [CITY]' naturally.
prompts.tip: Event planning directory profiles with a clear specialty ('we specialize in intimate weddings under 100 guests') attract better-fit inquiries than generalist descriptions.
